task_completion.description: Open the address book or contacts application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An address book or contacts application window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a new contact.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Contact', 'Add Contact', or similar data entry form/dialog is visible on the screen.
The text 'John Doe' is typed into the name or display name input field of the contact form.
The text 'john.doe@example.com' is typed into the email input field of the contact form.

task_completion.description: Save the contact and verify it appears in the address book.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contact 'John Doe' with the email 'john.doe@example.com' is visible as a saved entry in the main contacts list or in a structured contact details view, indicating the save action was successful.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to add the contact.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GUI buttons and text fields, rather than terminal commands (e.g., no sqlite3 or vcard CLI commands used to bypass the UI).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a real address book application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface belongs to a dedicated contacts/address book application (like GNOME Contacts or an email client's address book), not a generic text editor.

integrity.description: The final contact is displayed in a structured UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved contact details are rendered within the application's specific UI elements for contacts, rather than just being plain text typed into a blank document.
